message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Remdesivir (RDV) is the only antiviral drug so far approved for COVID-19 therapy by the FDA. However its efficacy is limited
                  <jats:italic>in vivo</jats:italic>
                  due to its low stability in presence of plasma. This paper compared the stability of RDV encapsulated with our platform technology based polymer NV-387 (NV-CoV-2), in presence of plasma
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  and
                  <jats:italic>in vivo</jats:italic>
                  . Furthermore, a non- clinical pharmacology studies of NV-CoV-2 (Polymer) and NV-CoV-2-R (Polymer encapsulated
                  <jats:italic>Remdesivir</jats:italic>
                  ) in both NL-63 infected and uninfected rats were done. In an
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  cell culture model experiment, antiviral activity of NV-CoV-2 and NV-CoV-2-R are also compared with RDV.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  The results are (i) NV-CoV-2 polymer encapsulation protects RDV from plasma- mediated catabolism
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  and
                  <jats:italic>in vivo</jats:italic>
                  , too. (ii) Body weight measurements of the normal (uninfected) rats after administration of the test materials (NV-CoV-2, and NV-CoV-2-R) show no toxic effects on them. (iii) NL-63 infected rats body weights and their survival length were like uninfected rats after treatment with NV-CoV-2 and NV-CoV-2-R, and the efficacy as an antiviral regimen were found in the order as below: NV-CoV-2-R > NV-CoV-2 > RDV.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>In brief, our platform technology based NV-387-encapsulated-RDV (NV-CoV-2-R) drug has a dual effect on coronaviruses. First, NV-CoV-2 itself as an antiviral regimen. Secondly, RDV is protected from plasma-mediated degradation in transit, rendering altogether the safest and an efficient regimen against COVID-19.</jats:p>
title: Dual Effects of Nanoviricides Platform Technology Based NV-CoV-2 Biomimetic Polymer Against COVID-19
